trip.name: Chill Food & Culture in Hong Kong

days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring Kowloon and tasting local delights

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: After landing, follow the signs to immigration, collect your bags, and take the Airport Express train to Kowloon station, a quick and efficient journey.

timeline.title: Check in to Harbour Plaza 8 Degrees
timeline.detail: Conveniently located just a short walk from the train station, this hotel offers comfortable rooms and excellent amenities for your stay.
timeline.mapQuery: Harbour Plaza 8 Degrees, Kowloon, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Temple Street Night Market
timeline.detail: Wander through the vibrant streets of Temple Street Night Market. Here you can shop for souvenirs, enjoy street performances, and sample local snacks.
timeline.mapQuery: Temple Street Night Market, Kowloon, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Try the famous wonton noodles at this renowned eatery. Known for its traditional recipes and thin, springy noodles, it's a must-visit for noodle lovers.

timeline.title: Victoria Harbour Night View
timeline.detail: Enjoy the stunning evening skyline of Hong Kong from the Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade. Don’t forget your camera for an unforgettable photospot.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, Kowloon, Hong Kong
timeline.tip: Best to visit after sunset for the lights to illuminate the skyline.

localTips: Get an Octopus card at the MTR station for convenient travel on public transport.
Try local snacks like egg waffles and fish balls at the night market.
Dress casually and be prepared for a bustling atmosphere in the markets.

days.title: Culture and Hiking on Lantau Island
days.subtitle: A day of exploration and nature

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Indulge in a classic Hong Kong-style breakfast of toast, eggs, and milk tea at this local favorite known for its quick service.
timeline.mapQuery: Australia Dairy Company, Kowloon, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Take MTR to Tung Chung
timeline.detail: Board the MTR from Kowloon to Tung Chung station, the gateway to Lantau Island. Enjoy the scenic ride and prepare for a day of adventure.
timeline.mapQuery: Tung Chung Station, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit the Big Buddha
timeline.detail: Upon arrival, hike the stairs to the Tian Tan Buddha statue, one of the world's largest seated Buddhas. Explore the nearby Po Lin Monastery and soak in the serene surroundings.
timeline.mapQuery: Tian Tan Buddha, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Vegetarian Restaurant, Po Lin Monastery
timeline.detail: Savor a peaceful vegetarian meal at the monastery's restaurant, known for its healthy and delicious dishes inspired by traditional Buddhist cuisine.
timeline.mapQuery: Po Lin Monastery, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore Ngong Ping Village
timeline.detail: After lunch, stroll around Ngong Ping Village. Check out the quaint shops and exhibits before taking the Ngong Ping 360 cable car for stunning views.
timeline.mapQuery: Ngong Ping Village, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Return to Kowloon
timeline.detail: Take the MTR back to Kowloon, unwind, and prepare for a relaxing evening after a day of exploration.

timeline.title: Dinner at a Local Cha Chaan Teng
timeline.detail: Visit a traditional Hong Kong tea restaurant for dinner. Try local specialties like baked pork chop rice or Hong Kong-style French toast.
timeline.mapQuery: Cha Chaan Teng, Kowloon, Hong Kong

localTips: The best time to visit the Big Buddha is in the morning before crowds arrive.
Wear comfortable shoes for the hiking trails around Lantau Island.
Consider booking the Ngong Ping 360 tickets in advance to skip long lines.

days.title: Shopping and Departure
days.subtitle: Last-minute shopping and cultural experiences

timeline.title: Breakfast at the Hotel
timeline.detail: Start the day with a hearty breakfast at your hotel. Take this time to relax and prepare for your last day in Hong Kong.
timeline.mapQuery: Harbour Plaza 8 Degrees, Kowloon, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Shopping at Ladies Market
timeline.detail: Explore the bustling Ladies Market in Mong Kok, a perfect spot to pick up souvenirs and local crafts. Don’t hesitate to bargain for the best prices.
timeline.mapQuery: Ladies Market, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Enjoy a meal at the world-famous dim sum restaurant known for its Michelin-starred dishes. Be sure to try the baked buns with BBQ pork.
timeline.mapQuery: Tim Ho Wan, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Immerse yourself in local culture at Wong Tai Sin Temple, renowned for its beautiful architecture and fortune telling. A peaceful conclusion to your trip.
timeline.mapQuery: Wong Tai Sin Temple, Kowloon, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Head back to Hotel and Check-out
timeline.detail: Return to the hotel, collect your luggage, and check out in time for your evening flight. Reflect on the memorable experiences of your trip.
timeline.mapQuery: Harbour Plaza 8 Degrees, Kowloon, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Depart for Airport
timeline.detail: Take the Airport Express back to Hong Kong International Airport, allowing plenty of time for your flight home.

localTips: Don't miss the chance to try egg tarts at any local bakery.
Purchase MTR tokens for ease of transit during your last day.
Check the timing for the fortune teller at Wong Tai Sin Temple for an authentic experience.

hotels.name: Harbour Plaza 8 Degrees
hotels.note: Modern hotel located within walking distance of Kowloon Station.
hotels.address: 199 Kowloon City Road, Tokwawan, Kowloon, Hong Kong
